1,Onboarding,Splash Screen,"1. Admin must have the valid URL of the portal.
2. Admin must be on the landing page of the portal.
","The center of the screen will feature the app's logo.
Below the logo, the app name ""FocusPilot.AI"" will be displayed in large, bold, orange letters.
A tagline, ""Navigate Your Goals. Achieve with Calm,"" will be presented below the app name in a smaller, simple black font
","1. The splash screen must be displayed for exactly 2.5 seconds each time the app is launched.
2. The splash screen should load immediately upon app launch to minimize user wait time.
3. The logo, app name, and tagline must be clearly visible and centered on the screen.
4. The splash screen should not be interactive, ensuring users focus on the branding elements.
5. The transition from the splash screen to the main app interface should be smooth and without delay.
6. The splash screen must be compatible with various screen sizes and resolutions to ensure a consistent user experience across devices.
","1. Upon launching the app, the splash screen must appear within 0.5 second

screen must be compatible with various screen sizes and resolutions to ensure a consistent user experience across devices.
","1. Upon launching the app, the splash screen must appear within 0.5 seconds.
2. The splash screen must remain visible for a full 2.5 seconds before transitioning to the main app interface.
3. The transition from the splash screen to the main interface should be smooth, with no noticeable lag or delay.
4. The app's logo, name, and tagline must be clearly visible and centered during the entire duration of the splash screen display.
","1. If the splash screen fails to load, a default error message should be displayed, indicating a loading issue.
2. The app should validate that all visual elements are correctly loaded before displaying the splash screen.
3. The app should ensure that the splash screen duration is precisely 2.5 seconds, with no deviation.
4. The app should validate that the transition from the splash screen to the main interface is seamless and without interruption.
"

2,Onboarding,Tutorial Screen,"1. The user has successfully launched the Focuspilot.ai app and navigated past the splash screen.
2. The app is installed on a device with an active internet connection.
3. The user is accessing the app for the first time.
4. The device's operating system supports the app's current version.
","1.A ""Skip"" button is located at the top-right corner, allowing users to bypass the tutorial.
2. The main content area features an Image related to the title and description.
3. Progress dots beneath the text indicate the user's current position in the tutorial sequence.
4. An action button at the bottom of the screen reads ""Next"" on the first two screens and ""Continue"" on the last screen.
","1. The tutorial screens must automatically appear after the splash screen for first-time users.
2. Each screen should display an image and text relevant to the app's main features.
3. Users should be able to navigate through the tutorial using a carousel layout, swiping left or right.
4. The ""Next"" button will take the user to the next screen, and the ""Continue"" button on the last screen will end the tutorial..
5. The ""Skip"" button should allow

layout, swiping left or right.
4. The ""Next"" button will take the user to the next screen, and the ""Continue"" button on the last screen will end the tutorial..
5. The ""Skip"" button should allow users to exit the tutorial at any point, taking them directly to the app's main interface.
","1. Upon launching the app, the tutorial screens should appear immediately after the splash screen.
2. Users should be able to swipe left or right to navigate between tutorial screens.
3. Tapping the ""Next"" button should transition the user to the next screen with a smooth animation.
4. The ""Skip"" button should be functional on all screens, redirecting users to the app's main interface.
5. On the final tutorial screen, the ""Continue"" button should be prominently displayed, leading users to the main app dashboard.
6. Progress dots should accurately reflect the user's current position in the tutorial sequence.
","1. The app should validate the presence of an active internet connection before loading tutorial content.
2. If a user taps ""Next"" or ""Continue"" without an active connection, an error message should inform them of the connectivity issue.
3. The app should ensure that all tutori

ading tutorial content.
2. If a user taps ""Next"" or ""Continue"" without an active connection, an error message should inform them of the connectivity issue.
3. The app should ensure that all tutorial content is loaded correctly before allowing navigation to the next screen.

3,Onboarding,Sign-In,"1. The user has tapped on the ""Skip"" or ""Continue"" CTA on the tutorial screen.
2. The application is installed and running on a compatible mobile device.
3. The user has an existing account with Focuspilot.ai.
4. The device is connected to the internet.
","1. The screen displays a bold ""Sign In"" title at the top, indicating the screen's purpose.
2. Below the title, a prompt instructs users to enter their email address.
3. Two input fields are present: one for the email ID with placeholder text ""Enter Email ID"" and another for the password with placeholder text ""Enter Password*"".
4. An eye icon is located next to the password field, allowing users to toggle password visibility.
5. A ""Forget Password?"" link is positioned to the right of the password field, offering password recovery options.
6. A checkbox is available for users to agree to the Terms and Conditions and Privacy

A ""Forget Password?"" link is positioned to the right of the password field, offering password recovery options.
6. A checkbox is available for users to agree to the Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy, with clickable links for each.
7. A ""Sign In"" button is displayed, initially disabled and greyed out, becoming active and orange when conditions are met.
8. A ""Create Account"" link is available for new users.
9. An alternative sign-in option is provided with a ""Continue with Apple"" button, featuring the Apple logo.
","1. The Sign In screen must validate that both the email and password fields are filled before enabling the ""Sign In"" button.
2. Users must check the agreement checkbox to enable the ""Sign In"" button, ensuring compliance with terms and conditions.
3. The system should allow users to view password using the eye icon for ease of input verification.
4. The ""Forget Password?"" link should redirect users to a password recovery screen, facilitating account access recovery.
5. The ""Create Account"" link should navigate users to the account creation process, expanding the user base.

recovery.
5. The ""Create Account"" link should navigate users to the account creation process, expanding the user base.
6. The system should provide an error message ""Invalid email address. Please try again."" for incorrect email formats.
7. An error message ""Wrong password. Please try again."" should appear for incorrect password entries.
8. The ""Continue with Apple"" option should authenticate users via their Apple ID, offering a streamlined login process.
","1. Users must enter a valid email and password to enable the ""Sign In"" button.
2. Upon checking the agreement checkbox, the ""Sign In"" button becomes active.
3. Tapping the ""Sign In"" button with valid credentials should authenticate the user and navigate them to the app's main dashboard.
4. Selecting the ""Forget Password?"" link should redirect users to the password recovery screen.
5. Tapping the ""Create Account"" link should initiate the account creation process.
6. Choosing the ""Continue with Apple"" button should authenticate the user via Apple ID and grant access to the app.
7. Error messages should display prominently when incorrect credentials are entered, guiding users to correct their input.

","1. The system should validate the email format and display ""Invalid email address. Please try again."" for incorrect formats.
2. Password entries should be masked by default, with the option to reveal them using the eye icon.
3. An error message ""Wrong password. Please try again."" should appear for incorrect password entries.

4,Onboarding, Forgot Password,"1. The user must have an existing account with a registered email address in the Focuspilot.ai app.
2. The user must be on the Sign In screen and tap the ""Forgot Password"" link to access this feature.
3. The device must be connected to the internet to send and receive OTP emails.
","1. A back arrow icon is present on the left side of the page header, allowing users to navigate back to the previous screen.
2. The page header displays the title ""Forgot Password"" centrally in bold.
3. Instructional text guides the user to enter their registered email address.
4. An email input field is labeled ""Email ID"" with a placeholder example email.
5. An ""Send OTP"" button is positioned below the email input field.
6. A message informs the user that a verification code has been sent to their email.
7. Six rectangular boxes are provided for OTP input, accompanied by a timer icon with a countdown.
8. A ""Submit"" button becomes active upon entering the full OTP.
9. Alternative action text offers a ""Resend"" option if the OTP is not received.

","1. The system must validate the entered email address against registered accounts.
2. Upon clicking ""Send OTP,"" the system should generate and send a 6-digit OTP to the user's email.
3. The OTP input field must accept only numeric input and display a countdown timer of 2 minutes.
4. The ""Submit"" button should remain inactive until a complete 6-digit OTP is entered.
5. If the OTP is incorrect, the system must display an error message prompting the user to re-enter the OTP.
6. After the timer expires, a ""Resend OTP"" link should appear, allowing the user to request a new OTP.
7. The system must handle multiple OTP requests and ensure each OTP is unique and valid for a single use.
8. Error messages must be displayed for invalid or unregistered email addresses.

. The user enters their registered email address and clicks ""Send OTP.""
3. The system sends an OTP to the entered email and displays a message confirming the action.
4. The user enters the 6-digit OTP in the provided input field.
5. The ""Submit"" button becomes active once the full OTP is entered.
6. The user clicks ""Submit,"" and the system verifies the OTP.
7. If the OTP is correct, the user is redirected to a password reset screen.
8. If the OTP is incorrect, an error message prompts the user to re-enter the OTP.
9. After 2 minutes, if the OTP is not entered, a ""Resend OTP"" link appears.
10. The user can click ""Resend OTP"" to receive a new OTP and restart the process.

8,Onboarding,Profile Setup,"1. The user's email must be successfully verified.
2. The user must be redirected to the Complete Profile screen.
3. The application must be in a state where it can display the profile completion form.
4. The user must have access to a stable internet connection.
","1. The header section includes a bold title ""Complete Your Profile"" and a subtitle with placeholder text.
2. The form contains four fields: Full Name, Age, Gender, and Occupation.
- Full Name: A single-line text field with a placeholder ""Full Name"".
- Age: A text fi

and a subtitle with placeholder text.
2. The form contains four fields: Full Name, Age, Gender, and Occupation.
- Full Name: A single-line text field with a placeholder ""Full Name"".
- Age: A text field with a placeholder ""Enter Age"" and a calendar icon for date selection.
- Gender: A dropdown menu with a placeholder ""Select Gender"" and options for Male, Female, and Other.
- Occupation: A dropdown menu with a placeholder ""Select Occupation"" and a comprehensive list of industry options.
3. The ""Continue"" button is initially gray and disabled, becoming orange and active once all fields are completed.
","1. The module must ensure that all four fields are mandatory and must be completed before proceeding.
2. The Age field should trigger a date picker when the calendar icon is tapped, allowing users to select their date of birth.
3. The Gender dropdown must provide options for Male, Female, and Other, ensuring inclusivity.

5. The ""Continue"" button must remain disabled until all fields are filled correctly, preventing incomplete submissions.
6. Upon successful completion of the form, a success pop-up should confirm the profile completion, enhancing user satisfaction.

9,Onboarding,Questionnaire,"1. The user must have successfully completed the profile setup screens.
2. The user must be logged into the Focuspilot.ai application.
3. The application must be connected to the internet to ensure data synchronization.
","1. A header section includes the title ""Questionnaire"" on the top left and a ""Skip All"" button on the top right.
2. A progress indicator with seven small square boxes is located below the header, highlighting completed sections with an orange line.
3. Each question is presented with a bolded prompt at the top of the screen.
4. Options are displayed as radio buttons for single-choice questions and checkboxes for multiple-choice questions.
5. Bottom navigation includes a ""Previous"" button on the left and a ""Save & Next"" button on the right.
","1. The questionnaire must present a series of questions designed to capture user preference

. Bottom navigation includes a ""Previous"" button on the left and a ""Save & Next"" button on the right.
","1. The questionnaire must present a series of questions designed to capture user preferences, motivations, and challenges.
2. Users should have the ability to skip the entire questionnaire by selecting the ""Skip All"" button.
3. Each question should allow users to proceed without answering by selecting the ""Save & Next"" button.
4. Users must be able to navigate back to the previous question using the ""Previous"" button.
5. For the first two questions, users are restricted to selecting only one option(Radio button)
6. For subsequent questions, users can select up to two options to provide more nuanced responses(Checkbox).
7. The system must store user responses in real-time to ensure data is not lost if the session is interrupted.
8. The questionnaire should adapt to user inputs, providing relevant follow-up questions based on previous answers.
","1. Users must be able to access the questionnaire after completing the profile setup.
2. The ""Skip All"" button should allow users to bypass the entire questionnaire and proceed to the main app interface.
3. Each question must

ess the questionnaire after completing the profile setup.
2. The ""Skip All"" button should allow users to bypass the entire questionnaire and proceed to the main app interface.
3. Each question must be displayed sequentially, with users able to navigate using the ""Previous"" and ""Save & Next"" buttons. All the questions will be uploaded from the Admin Panel.
4. The system should highlight the current question in the progress indicator.
5. For single-choice questions, selecting an option should automatically deselect any previously selected option.
6. For multiple-choice questions, users should be able to select up to two options, with the system preventing additional selections.
","1. The system must validate that only one option is selected for single-choice questions.
2. For multiple-choice questions, the system should validate that no more than two options are selected.
"

3. The app must be installed on a device with location services enabled.

2. The user must be logged into the FocusPilot app.
3. The app must be installed on a device with location services enabled.
4. The device must have an active internet connection.","1. The pop-up will prominently feature the prompt ""Allow 'FocusPilot' to access your location?"" in bold text.
2. Three vertically arranged buttons will be available: ""Allow while using App,"" ""Allow Once,"" and ""Don’t Allow,"" with the last option highlighted in red for emphasis.","1. The system must prompt the user for location access immediately after the questionnaire section is completed or skipped.
2. The ""Allow while using App"" option should enable location access only when the app is actively in use, ensuring user privacy.
3. The ""Allow Once"" option should grant temporary location access for the current session, requiring the user to grant permission again in future sessions.
4. The ""Don’t Allow"" option should completely deny location access, preventing the app from fetching the user's location.
5. The app must store the user's choice securely and respect their privacy preferences in all subsequent interactions.
6. The app should be able to handle changes in location permissions dyna

ocation.
5. The app must store the user's choice securely and respect their privacy preferences in all subsequent interactions.
6. The app should be able to handle changes in location permissions dynamically, updating its features and recommendations accordingly.","1. Upon completing or skipping the questionnaire, the user is automatically redirected to the Location Access page.
